news.title: ChatGPT uninstalls surged by 295% after DoD deal
news.summary: Many consumers ditched ChatGPT's app after news of its DoD deal went live, while Claude's downloads grew.

news.title: Stripe wants to turn your AI costs into a profit center
news.summary: Stripe released a preview intended to allow AI companies to easily track, pass through, and make a profit on underlying AI model fees.

news.title: Geopolitical drama reportedly stalls IPO of SoftBank-backed PayPay
news.summary: The Japan-based company reportedly aimed for a public valuation of at least ¥1.5 trillion ($10 billion).

news.title: No one has a good plan for how AI companies should work with the government
news.summary: As OpenAI transitions from a wildly successful consumer startup into a piece of national security infrastructure, the company seems unequipped to manage its new responsibilities.

news.title: A new app alerts you if someone nearby is wearing smart glasses
news.summary: A hobbyist developer's new app, which can detect nearby smart glasses, comes amid resistance to always-on recording and listening devices that invade people's privacy.

news.title: Anthropic upgrades Claude’s memory to attract AI switchers
news.summary: Anthropic is making it easier to switch to its Claude AI from other chatbots with an update that brings Claude's memory feature to users on the free plan, along with a new prompt and dedicated tool for importing data from other chatbots. news.title: Apple’s cheapest AirPods have hit $89
news.summary: It’s a good day for Apple fans on a budget. Not only did the company introduce a new budget phone, its entry-level AirPods 4 are also on sale. news.title: How the new iPhone 17E stacks up against Apple’s pricier phones
news.summary: The iPhone 17 lineup now has a “budget” option. Announced this week, the iPhone 17E will hit stores on Wednesday, March 11th, starting at $599, with preorders opening on March 4th. news.title: Apple might use Google servers to store data for its upgraded AI Siri
news.summary: Apple has asked Google to look into "setting up servers" for a new version of Siri that's Gemini-powered and meets Apple's privacy requirements, The Information reports. news.summary: Sharing content to Mastodon is getting easier with the addition of a universal "Share to Mastodon" button for the decentralized social media platform. news.title: Clueless cops post seized crypto wallet password. $5M quickly stolen.
news.summary: South Korean police deeply apologized for preventable loss of seized funds.

news.title: Charter gets FCC permission to buy Cox and become largest ISP in the US
news.summary: FCC rejects protests because Charter and Cox don't compete directly in most places.

news.title: Iowa county adopts strict zoning rules for data centers, but residents still worry
news.summary: Though the rules are among the strictest in the US, locals say they aren't enough.

news.title: $599 M4 iPad Air is a lot like the old one, but with a substantial RAM boost
news.summary: Unexpected RAM upgrade is the highlight of an otherwise straightforward refresh.

news.title: Research roundup: Six cool science stories we almost missed
news.summary: Smart underwear measures farts, brain cells play Doom, and AI discovers rules of an ancient game.

news.title: What Is That Mysterious Metallic Device US Chief Design Officer Joe Gebbia Is Using?
news.summary: Gebbia was reportedly spotted at a San Francisco coffee shop using an unidentified pair of earbuds with a circular disc that looks similar to the device seen in a recent OpenAI hoax ad.

news.title: The ‘European’ Jolla Phone Is an Anti-Big-Tech Smartphone
news.summary: The Finnish company Jolla is back with the Linux-powered Jolla Phone. It's being positioned as an antidote to the US-dominated smartphone status quo of Android and iOS.

news.title: This AI Agent Is Ready to Serve, Mid-Phone Call
news.summary: Deutsche Telekom, the German cell provider—which holds a majority stake in T-Mobile—is partnering with ElevenLabs to enable an AI assistant on all of its network’s calls in Germany. No app required.

news.title: Missile Attacks Are Overwhelming the Gulf. Delivery Drivers Are Still on the Roads
news.summary: Missile and drone attacks have disrupted daily life, but delivery drivers are still diligently navigating streets to drop off orders across the region.

news.title: Iranian Ayatollah Khamenei’s Death Sparks Revolt Among Kalshi Customers
news.summary: How Kalshi decided to settle a market tied to Iran’s supreme leader prompted anger among traders who felt the rules weren’t clear.
